danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen numerical countdown; secondary countdown appears at top of screen when less than one minute remains
danger_signals.meaning: Temporal pressure and imminent failure state; running out of time results in loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar changes color based on remaining health percentage
danger_signals.meaning: Visual feedback on damage accumulation and proximity to death; color transition indicates increasing danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Turtle Terrorists and other hostile entities positioned throughout environment
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or engagement; indicates contested territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental features that lead to 'harmful objects' and level bifurcations with risk
danger_signals.meaning: Spatial danger zones requiring navigation skill; world-state of unstable or hostile terrain

danger_signals.visual_form: Final level in each episode features distinct boss entity (visual prominence differs from standard enemies)
danger_signals.meaning: Escalation of danger; world-state shifting to confrontation phase with authority figure

safety_signals.visual_form: Interactive food item collectible in level space
safety_signals.meaning: Immediate health restoration; one hit's worth of recovery; signals safe resource availability
Temporary invulnerability state; blocks one or four hits; signals damage mitigation opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Shootable sign object placed in level progression path
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respawn point established; world-state of progress secured; death consequences mitigated

safety_signals.visual_form: Star particle effects surrounding Jazz during invincibility state
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility active; danger window closed; world-state of temporary immunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Shootable goal marker at level conclusion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone and level completion; escape from danger state; world-state transition to success

safety_signals.visual_form: Life items available through collection or bonus completion
safety_signals.meaning: Failure consequences mitigated; reserve safety margin; world-state of security buffer increased

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Carrotus planet home base; peaceful native world aesthetics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tortoise/turtle imagery; mastermind tortoise Devan Shell as visual authority figure
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Turtle Terrorists appear as hostile unit type; distinct enemy visual design from other creatures
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Conquered planets under occupation; distant airbase as hidden command center; cloning machine facility

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Planet-specific native creatures and enemies reflect occupied world identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each conquered planet retains native visual aesthetic while under occupation; visual blend of original and Devan's control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level environments show conquered planets with mixed aesthetic—original world identity degraded by occupation forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies present throughout levels; hostile activity normalized; native creatures replaced or displaced by Turtle Terrorists

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final level in each episode features distinct boss arena; visual escalation and environmental prominence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single major threat requiring direct confrontation; heightened aggression from authority figure; narrative checkpoint

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distant airbase environment design; hidden facility aesthetics; visual separation from main worlds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Devan escapes confrontation; cloning machine present; indicates deeper threat layer; world-state of incomplete resolution

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scattered weapons, ammunition in enclosed pickups requiring collection; food items indicate survival focus
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ammunition availability limited and segmented; players must actively extract resources from environment; competitive resource gathering

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Finish sign marker; transition between episodes with story advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level-end checkpoint triggers boss progression; successful finish grants time bonus and perfect score opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer visual; secondary timer at top screen appears when < 1 minute remains; visual urgency increase
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty tier affects timer length (Hard/Turbo shorter); failure consequence triggers if time expires; forces speed-run mentality

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Question mark sign (replaces Devan Shell portrait) indicates secret level access points
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Secret levels unlock in specific areas; represent hidden world state; alternative progression paths available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pseudo-3D third-person camera shift; speedway visual aesthetic change; blue diamond collection objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Switches from 2D platforming to 3D first-person speed mechanic; alternate challenge type; extra life reward

environmental_storytelling.example: Conquered planets retain native aesthetic elements but show signs of Turtle Terrorist occupation and control infrastructure
Turtle Terrorists as visually distinct enemy faction conveying occupation force; other planet-native enemies show indigenous identity
Distant airbase design signals hidden enemy command center; visual separation from conquered worlds indicates strategic importance
Weapons enclosed in pickup structures suggest scarcity under occupation; ammunition distribution implies contested resource control
Shootable sign objects mark safe progress zones; visual waypoints indicate heroic journey through dangerous territory
Jazz bird form (Hip Hop sidekick) and lizard form indicate environmental adaptation; alternative gameplay perspective conveys world diversity
Countdown timer becomes more visually prominent when critical; spatial layout forced into speed-run interpretation under temporal stress
Multiple routes through levels with question mark signs indicate resistance movement infrastructure; secret routes suggest underground knowledge

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Increased run speed and jump height the longer continuous running occurs; rapid movement and traversal capability
Health bar color shift visible; movement capability unchanged but visual warning system active; proximity to death indicated
Yellow stars particle effect display; temporary immunity state; aggressive movement capability maximized; danger window closed
Secondary countdown timer appears at screen top; visual urgency escalation; behavioral pressure toward speed-run execution
Secret level exclusive transformation; flight capability; mini-boss engagement mechanic; distinct gameplay rules
Secret level exclusive transformation (episode C); distinct movement and combat capability; world-state diversity signal
Boss encounter at episode finale; high visual prominence; single major threat requiring direct engagement; escape capability after defeat
Escapes with kidnapped princess Eva after confrontation; deploys cloning machine as secondary threat; indicates deeper threat layer
Present throughout conquered planet levels; hostile but standard encounter; occupied territory population; non-boss engagement
Indigenous creatures present in occupied worlds; visual identity retained but behavioral role shifted to obstacles; indicate native world identity beneath occupation

notes: Jazz Jackrabbit uses visual state communication primarily through color shifting (health bar), particle effects (invincibility stars), timer urgency escalation, and environmental markers (checkpoint signs, finish signs, secret question marks). World-state is conveyed through occupational layering—conquered planets visually retain native aesthetics while showing Turtle Terrorist occupation presence. Faction identity relies on animal symbolism (hares vs. tortoises) and color coding (Jazz green). Political situation manifests as spatial occupation with hidden command center, resistance route availability, and kidnapped princess narrative urgency. Danger signals are temporal (countdown) and health-based (color shift); safety signals are item-based (carrots, shields, extra lives) and spatial (checkpoints, finish signs). The game communicates world-state through mechanic-visual pairing: time pressure visible increase, resource scarcity through pickup enclosure, and narrative progression through episode completion markers.
